content.full_text:
.t

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance/Administration       •   •   •      Executive/Legal      •   •   •      Enforcement   •   •   •      Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                                322-5901                                322-6441                    322-6444

December 15, 1977

77-12-083

Bruce Comly French
Staff Director and Counsel
Committee on Governmental
Operations
Council of the District of
Columbia
Washington, D.C.  20004

Dear Mr. French:

     This is in response to your letter to the Commission dated
November 11, 1977, requesting an interpretation of California
Government Code Section 87100. As I understand your question,
you wish to know whether an official may vote on matters which
would foreseeably provide the official with a higher salary.
Such a vote, or participation in such a matter, would not con-
stitute a conflict of interest under the California Political
Reform Act for the following reasons.

     Sections 87100 and 87103 provide, in relevant part:

     87100. No public official at any level of state
or local government shall make, participate in
making or in any way attempt to use his official
position to influence a governmental decision in
which he knows or has reason to know he has a
financial interest.

     87103. An official has a financial interest in
a decision within the meaning of Section 87100
if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision
will have a material financial effect, distin-
guishable from its effect on the public generally,
on:

          (c) Any source of income, other than loans by
a commercial lending institution in the regular
course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value received by or
promised to the public official within twelve
months prior to the time when the decision is
made;.

Mr. Bruce Comley French
December 15, 1977
Page Two

     With reference to Section 87103(c), supra, in determining
whether something is a "source of income", it is necessary to
analyze the definition of "income" found in Government Code
Section 82030. That section specifically provides that:

     ..."Income" shall also not include:

          (2) Salary and reimbursement for expenses
     or per diem received from a state or local govern-
     ment agency and reimbursement for travel expenses
     and per diem received from a bona fide educational,
     academic or charitable organization.

     Furthermore, pursuant to its rule-making power, the Fair
Political Practices Commission has promulgated a Regulation
defining the concept "making or participating in the making of
a governmental decision" as it appears in Section 87100. That
Regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(d)(3), provides in
relevant part:

     (d) Making or participating in the making of
a governmental decision shall not include:
          ...
          (3) Actions by public officials, employees,
or employee representatives relating to their
compensation or the terms or conditions of their
employment or contract.

     Accordingly, it does not appear that the situation you
hypothesize would create a conflict of interest under California
law.

     I have enclosed some general materials concerning California
conflict of interest statutes which I hope may prove helpful to
you in the future. Thank you for writing the Commission and please
forgive our delay in responding. If I can be of further assistance
in this or any other matter, please feel free to call on me at
(916)322-6444.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist III
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:mfa
Enclosure

77083

COUNCIL OF THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA

WASHINGTON D C 20004

November 11, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
State Office Building
Sacramento, CA. 95814

Dear Commission:

The Council of the District of Columbia, the local
legislature for the District of Columbia, created by an Act
of Congress, is considering legislation to amend a provision
of the elections laws which prohibits an elected official
from seeking another elected office which term overlaps with
his or her current term.

Under the provisions of the District's conflict of
interest law, a Member of the Council must disqualify himself
or herself from voting or considering a matter in which he
or she stands to have financial gain, e.g. higher salary in
the new office, plus salary as an incumbent while seeking the
higher office. Other members whose terms coincide with that
of the new office sought may run as an elected incumbent.
Parenthically, the determination of terms was initially made
by drawing lots when the first Council took office on January
2, 1975.

Under the provisions of California Government Code Sec.
87100 et seq. would a vote by a Member under such circumstances
be allowed?

I inquire of your office in light of your well observed
national reputation as a front runner in this area, and in light
of your comprehensive and narrowly drawn Initiative law concerning
elections. Should you have further questions, please feel free to
call me collect at 202-724-8116.

Yours cordially,

BRUCE COMLY FRENCH
Staff Director and Counsel
Committee on Government Operations

BCF:ia

BRUCE COMLY FRENCH
Staff Director and Counsel
Committee on Government Operations

sections.question: Under the provisions of California Government Code Sec. 87100 et seq. would a vote by a Member under such circumstances be allowed? [where a District of Columbia Council Member seeks another elected office with overlapping terms and stands to gain financial benefit (higher salary) while remaining an incumbent]
sections.conclusion: The verbatim conclusion section is not clearly preserved in the provided document due to image quality/OCR issues, but the FPPC response addresses conflict of interest disqualification requirements under Section 87100.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a District of Columbia Council Member may vote on legislation affecting election laws when the Member stands to gain financial benefit (higher salary) by seeking another elected office with an overlapping term, under California Government Code Section 87100 et seq.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A public official must disqualify themselves from voting or considering a matter in which they have a financial interest, including potential salary gains from seeking overlapping elected office, consistent with Section 87100 conflict of interest provisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document quality issues significantly impact extraction accuracy. The incoming request letter from D.C. Council is complete and clear, but the FPPC's response pages are heavily damaged/degraded with substantial OCR errors and missing text. The document is clearly a correspondence between D.C. Council and California FPPC, with the request fully readable but the FPPC's formal advice conclusion only partially recoverable. Confidence reduced due to incomplete conclusion text in the response portion.

QUESTION: Under the provisions of California Government Code Sec. 87100 et seq. would a vote by a Member under such circumstances be allowed? [where a District of Columbia Council Member seeks another elected office with overlapping terms and stands to gain financial benefit (higher salary) while remaining an incumbent]

CONCLUSION: The verbatim conclusion section is not clearly preserved in the provided document due to image quality/OCR issues, but the FPPC response addresses conflict of interest disqualification requirements under Section 87100.

embedding.summary: The Council of the District of Columbia requested FPPC guidance on whether a Council Member may vote on election law amendments when seeking another elected office with overlapping terms that would provide financial gain. The FPPC responded regarding conflict of interest disqualification requirements under California Government Code Section 87100.
